verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, a canonical cytoplasmic/nuclear chaperone. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the plasma membrane outer leaflet under stress conditions (heat shock, oxidative stress, oncogenic transformation). Surface HSP70 is found on tumor cells and stressed cells, where it associates with the outer leaflet (possibly via lipid rafts/ceramide) and is recognized by NK cells and antibodies. This surface form is not present under baseline unstressed conditions in most normal cells, making it a context-dependent (stress/tumor-induced) surface presentation rather than a constitutive one.
